name: Kuo-t'ieu (Fried Pork Dumplings)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix warm water with flour and stir with a chopstick. Gradually add cold water and knead until well combined. Let rest for 15-30 minutes, covered.
2. In a bowl, mix pork, scallions, ginger, water chestnuts, salt, sesame oil, soy sauce, sherry, and cornstarch.
3. Divide the dough into 40-50 pieces. Flatten each piece with your hand and roll into 2-3 inch round thin pancakes.
4. Place 1 to 2 teaspoons of filling in the center of each pancake. Fold over to form a half-moon shape and pinch edges together.
5. Heat a flat pan until hot, then add 3 tablespoons vegetable oil. Arrange enough dumplings on the pan without overlapping so that they cover the bottom.
6. Cook for 2 minutes or until the bottoms turn golden brown. Add 2/3 cup cold water to the pan, cover, and cook until the water has evaporated.
7. Add 1 tablespoon sesame oil to the side of the pan and fry for another minute before serving.
Dipping Sauce:
Soy sauce, sesame oil, chopped scallions, and chopped ginger
